summary: Respell prose.ts's mention/include sentinels as unicode escape sequences so the file stays NUL-free text — same runtime string, grep no longer binary-detects it

edit.description: MENTION_SLOT (line 56) and INCLUDE_SLOT (line 64) are defined with literal control-byte string values (U+0000 and U+0001), so grep binary-detects the whole file and silently suppresses every match. Respell both literals as the `\u0000` / `\u0001` escape sequences — a byte-identical runtime string, the file becomes NUL-free text. Source-form only, behavior unchanged; the module doc/comments that spell the sentinel need no rewrite beyond the two constant lines.

acceptance: `grep MENTION_SLOT sdk/src/prose.ts` matches without `-a` (file no longer binary-detected); `pnpm --dir sdk test` green — runtime string byte-identical, existing prose tests cover behavior.
notes: One-line source-hygiene fix a human flagged via inbox; the NUL defeats the collaboration rule's grep-before-claiming probe every tick and no queued entry opens prose.ts to let it ride the next-open exit. No new test — runtime string unchanged. Friction capture .flume/friction/plan-grep-nul-binary-false-negative.md and open-questions' prose.ts sweep-mechanics warning retire in plan's post-ship audit (build cannot touch .flume/). scoped at ab3bcb8.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at 8575dd6 (ship audit): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
